title: Britain's Kerr breaks 27-year mile world record in London
description: Great Britain's Josh Kerr smashes the long-standing men's mile world record in a spectacular performance at the London Diamond League.
fullText:
Great Britain's Josh Kerr smashed the long-standing men's mile world record with a spectacular performance at the London Diamond League.

Having announced his intention back in March to break Hicham El Guerrouj's 27-year mark on home soil, Kerr delivered in emphatic fashion as he crossed the line in three minutes 42.66 seconds.

Roared on by a sell-out crowd of 60,000 at London Stadium, the five-time global medallist bettered the Moroccan's mark - untouched since 1999, when Kerr was aged one - by nearly half a second.

The 28-year-old became the seventh British athlete to claim the mile record, and first since Steve Cram in 1985.

Kerr had lined up as the sixth-fastest man in history over the mile with a British record personal best of 3:45.34.

He was joined on the start line in London by American Yared Nuguse, fourth on that all-time list and a genuine threat to the Olympic silver medallist's challenge.

But, in the end, it was only ever going to be Kerr.

Initially assisted by two pacemakers, who were both gone by the 1,000m mark, the Scot was peerless as he pressed on in pursuit of the wavelights on the curb of the track measuring his progress.

Bringing the crowd to their feet, Kerr went through 1500m faster than his British record over that distance and never wavered, with the emotion evident on his face as he raised his arms aloft in celebration.

"It's very overwhelming with the amount of hype [I created]. It's silly to call [the attempt] that early because there's a lot of things which can go wrong, but I am surrounded by amazing people and was able to stay consistent and put the work in," Kerr told BBC Sport.

"If I am to leave my mark on this sport as a British legend, following in the footsteps of the legends behind me, I have to put in those performances.

"Those performances take every single part of you, every single part of your team. The amount of work behind the scenes is incredible. Today it was a performance I was able to bring out - I just hoped it would be a little bit faster!"

Hodgkinson wins in London after 'tough couple of weeks'

Kerr, always unapologetically bold in declaring his ambitions, announced he would target the world record in London four months before this historic run - and has worked relentlessly towards backing that up since.

His coach, Danny Mackey, confirmed the Briton had set his sights on that goal since returning from the calf injury which ended his bid for gold at the World Championships in Tokyo last September.

The 28-year-old documented his preparations in the lead-up to Saturday's record attempt, titling his endeavours 'Project 222' in reference to the total number of seconds standing between him and history.

That culminated in a 1,200m time trial which suggested he was in the shape to achieve it, clocking 2:42.45 to leave him with more than one minute to cover the remaining 400m.

One caveat to that time is that Kerr did have a running start. However, he also recorded the time more than 5,000 feet above sea level in Albuquerque, and would not have the limiting effects of altitude to contend with on home soil.

In London, Kerr was kitted out in a bespoke speed suit and spikes created by sponsors Brooks, with the former designed to both enhance his aerodynamics and improve breathability in the warm temperatures through laser-cut perforations.

The spikes, designed to maximise his biomechanics, featured a carbon plate and aggressive rocker for propulsion, and titanium pins to keep the shoe light.

Having passed the 1,200m mark in 2:46.5, Kerr maintained his pace - clocking 13.7 seconds for each of the final three 100m splits - to once again back up his belief he could surpass El Guerrouj's time.

Kerr, whose training is based in the United States, decided to target the record this season as it was the first in six years not to culminate in a major global championship.

Over the preceding five years, he has established himself as one of the sport's biggest draws.

The Scot has twice made the Olympic podium and claimed three world titles - including a stunning 1500m triumph in Budapest in 2023 which ignited his heated rivalry with Norway's Jakob Ingebrigtsen, who is currently recovering from an Achilles injury.

Throughout that period, Kerr has remained vocal about his goals and self-belief - although even he admitted he may have gone slightly early in sharing hope of his latest target.

He will now seek to replicate his success at the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow next month.

"It felt like I had a kitchen full of amazing incredible chefs and I was like, 'what the hell are we going to make?' - and I was like, 'this is the dish I want to make, let's get to work!'," added Edinburgh-born Kerr.

"That's what it felt like. There was no point in going through this year thinking it was an off year, and we can wait until next year.

"It's about doubling down, building a performance to bring British athletics back to the mile to this stadium."

While not a distance contested at the Olympics or World Championships, the mile remains a revered event.

It has long stood as the ultimate benchmark of athletic performance, with a sub-four-minute mile considered impossible until Sir Roger Bannister famously became the first person to break that barrier in Oxford in 1954.

That returned the world record to British hands after Sydney Wooderson's mark was surpassed in 1942.

But Bannister would only hold the record for 46 days, before Derek Ibbotson reclaimed it for Britain in 1957, and Sebastian Coe, Steve Ovett and Cram ensured the world's best time remained in British hands between July 1979 and September 1993.

Algeria's Noureddine Morceli held the record for four years before his time was beaten by El Guerrouj in Rome.

"Josh is a world champion, he's won gold medals, run records but the world mile record perhaps surpasses everything," Cram said on BBC Sport.

"Just for what it means in the sport - it's an event the whole world knows and understands. Roger Bannister is partly responsible for that.

"To join that list of great names - I know when I did it, I so wanted to be in that club of world mile record holders. Welcome to the club, Josh!"

title: Barcelona will consider offers of €50-60 million for defender amid Bayern Munich interest
description: According to SPORT, FC Barcelona are open to listening to offers for Jules Kounde, but only if they reflect the French defender’s true market value. Although the French international is not consider...
fullText:
According to SPORT, FC Barcelona are open to listening to offers for Jules Kounde, but only if they reflect the French defender’s true market value.

Although the French international is not considered untouchable, Barcelona have no intention of making his departure easy, amid tentative interest from Bayern Munich.

The sporting department continues to rate the French international among the best right-backs in world football and would only consider selling him if an offer matched that status.

The report claims that Barcelona would be willing to study offers in the region of €50-60 million, a valuation the club believes accurately reflects Kounde’s quality and importance.

However, Bayern, who have shown interest in the defender, unwilling to reach that level. Indeed, the German champions are believed to value the defender at around €40 million, a figure the Blaugrana consider too low.

Barcelona value Kounde at €50-60 million. (Photo by Juan Manuel Serrano Arce/Getty Images)

As a result, there are currently no active negotiations between the clubs, only initial enquiries.

While Bayern admire Kounde, the significant gap between both clubs’ valuations means a transfer is far from becoming a realistic possibility.

Barcelona have not received any formal offer and remain under no pressure to sell. Unless another club meets their asking price, Kounde is expected to remain an important part of Hansi Flick’s squad.

Should Kounde’s situation eventually change, one player admired by Barcelona, as reported already, is Tottenham right-back Pedro Porro.

Barcelona like Pedro Porro. (Photo by Florencia Tan Jun/Getty Images)

The Spanish international has further enhanced his reputation with impressive World Cup performances after already establishing himself as one of the Premier League’s top full-backs.

Even so, a deal would be extremely difficult. After all, Porro recently signed a new contract with Tottenham, increasing the club’s negotiating strength.

For now, Kounde remains at Barcelona, with Bayern’s interest considered well below the level needed to trigger serious discussions.

The Frenchman may have endured a difficult last season, but showed his level at the World Cup, and Flick & co. will hope that version of the player shows up when he joins training with Barcelona in the coming weeks.

title: Rodri hails Messi as ‘the best player of all time’ ahead of World Cup final
description: Rodri says he is relishing coming up against “the best player of all time” Lionel Messi in the World Cup final.Spain face Argentina on Sunday with midfielder Rodri and his side looking to deny Arg...
fullText:
Rodri says he is relishing coming up against “the best player of all time” Lionel Messi in the World Cup final.

Spain face Argentina on Sunday with midfielder Rodri and his side looking to deny Argentina back-to-back World Cup titles.

But while the Manchester City man says stopping Messi will be key, he says Spain cannot pay too much attention to the 39-year-old.

“For me, he is the best player of all time, and he is a player who is capable of leading his national team and bringing them to Qatar to win the World Cup, and in this case, to the final,” Rodri said.

“But I think Argentina is much more than Messi. I think they have shown themselves to be a very complete team with very high-level players, and today, honestly, I think we are the two teams that play best collectively, so to speak. So, obviously we will have to take Leo into account, but also many others.”

Argentina again demonstrated their resilience by coming from behind to beat England – but Rodri says his side are prepared for the challenge.

“That obviously speaks of a very competitive character that national team has, of knowing how to overcome adverse situations. And it speaks of personality, and we take that into account, the type of team they are.

“We will try to hurt them in the way I believe we can hurt them. And the match can go through many circumstances, but as I told your colleague, you have to go for the match. You have to want to win the World Cup, and you have to be ambitious. And I think it comes down to being ourselves throughout the entire match.”

title: Real Madrid change stance on summer move for Man City superstar after World Cup excellence
description: Real Madrid are prepared to revive their pursuit of Rodri after Jose Mourinho identified the Manchester City midfielder as the player capable of transforming his new-look midfield, according to journa...
fullText:
Real Madrid are prepared to revive their pursuit of Rodri after Jose Mourinho identified the Manchester City midfielder as the player capable of transforming his new-look midfield, according to journalist Sergio Valentin.

The Spain international has been admired at the Santiago Bernabeu for some time, but concerns over his age, injury record and the size of any potential transfer fee had previously pushed the move into the background.

That position has now reportedly changed.

Valentin claims Mourinho has asked the club to sign a midfielder with a different profile to the options already in the squad, prompting Real Madrid to revisit a deal that had shown little movement until the closing stages of the World Cup.

The plan, according to the report, is to explore a move once the tournament concludes.

Real Madrid have changed their stance on Rodri. (Photo by Florencia Tan Jun/Getty Images)

Replacing the profiles of Toni Kroos and Luka Modric has been one of Real Madrid’s biggest long-term challenges.

While players such as Aurelien Tchouameni, Eduardo Camavinga and Federico Valverde offer energy, athleticism and defensive security, none naturally dictate the tempo of a game in the way Kroos did for almost a decade.

Mourinho believes the Manchester City star is the type of midfielder Real Madrid have been missing since Kroos’ departure, a player capable of controlling possession, organising attacks and giving structure to the team in difficult matches.

Fortunately for Los Blancos, Rodri himself would welcome a move to the Bernabeu, although any negotiations are expected to wait until after the World Cup.

Jose Mourinho wants to sign a new midfielder for Real Madrid. (Picture credit: X/@realmadrid)

Until recently, Madrid’s recruitment team had reservations about committing to such an expensive deal.

Rodri’s recent injury problems, combined with the significant fee Manchester City would be expected to demand for a player approaching his thirties, raised legitimate questions about whether the transfer represented value for money.

His contractual situation, however, has introduced a new dimension.

With Rodri now in the final year of his Manchester City contract, Real Madrid believe the conditions for a deal could become more favourable than they appeared earlier in the summer.

Even so, the transfer would almost certainly require movement in Madrid’s own squad before any formal approach could be made.

title: Grangeville's Adkison, WSU's Manzardo highlight local connections to pro baseball

fullText:
Jul. 18—When a door closes, another may open.

That's exactly what happened for 2019 Grangeville High School graduate Chase Adkison when he signed with the reigning World Series champion Los Angeles Dodgers' organization in June.

Following a long-term injury to Dodgers All-Star catcher Will Smith, L.A. initiated a chain reaction of catcher promotions and signings, eventually finding Adkison, recently released by the St. Louis Cardinals' organization.

Adkison reported to the Dodgers' High-A affiliate, the Great Lakes Loons in Midland, Mich, where through 13 games he has nine hits, five walks, a double, four RBI and nine runs.

In parts of four seasons in minor league baseball, Adkison is batting .268, with five home runs, 55 RBI and 56 runs.

The Grangeville grad found his way to professional baseball by way of the now-defunct Boise State baseball program in 2020, San Jacinto College (Texas) in 2021 and two standout seasons at Oklahoma State, where he started 98 total games and batted .282, with 11 home runs, 16 doubles, a triple, 41 walks, 69 RBI and 77 runs.

"This move is another opportunity to continue playing professional baseball," Adkison said in an Idaho County Free Press article. "I'm excited to be wearing a new uniform and have the chance to compete every day. I'm grateful for the opportunity and looking forward to helping the organization however I can."

Adkison holds four Grangeville High baseball records seven years after his career concluded, including single-season batting average (.653), single-season triples (eight), career hits (151) and career triples (20), according to the Idaho County Free Press.

Here's a look at other local connections to major and minor league baseball in 2026, including 10 Washington State Cougars and four Lewis-Clark State Warriors.

Connor Brogdon — RHP — Cleveland Guardians

With the Angels last season, Connor Brogdon signed in December with the Cleveland Guardians.

In 15 appearances, Brogdon has turned in a 2-2 record and 5.28 ERA. He has struck out 14 through 15 1/3 innings.

Seth Brown — OF — New York Yankees — Triple-A

Weeks before shuttling off to Florida for spring training, MLB veteran Seth Brown visited his alma mater Lewis-Clark State for a first-of-its-kind fundraising dinner.

The favor benefited his college coach, Jeremiah Robbins, who returned to LCSC this past season.

Brown spoke about how his time in Lewiston springboarded him to the career he has had. After seven years with the Athletics, Brown signed with the New York Yankees this past winter and is waiting for his opportunity.

With the Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre RailRiders, Brown has logged 53 games and is batting .235, with 43 hits, including six doubles, a triple and nine home runs en route to 21 RBI and 23 runs.

Drake George — RHP — San Francisco Giants — Single-A

After turning in a 3.20 ERA in Year 1, Drake George has been on the Giants org's injured list for the entire season so far.

Last year, George struck out 62 through 56 1/3 innings pitched.

Jadon Williamson — RHP — Miami Marlins — FCL Marlins

The most recent Warrior-turned-pro-baseball player on this list, Jadon Williamson has gotten two starts and made 11 total appearances for the Florida Complex League Marlins in 2026 after being selected in the 11th round of the 2025 MLB draft.

Williamson holds a 15.66 ERA, but he has struck out 24 batters through 16 2/3 innings pitched.

Kyle Manzardo — 1B/DH — Cleveland Guardians

WSU's former star slugger Kyle Manzardo has had a so-so year at the plate in 2026, but what transpired in his life over a year ago dwarfs any sort of success or failure he could experience between the lines.

In July 2025, Manzardo's mother, Windy Manzardo, underwent long-awaited heart transplant surgery. Kyle Manzardo flew from Houston, where the Cleveland Guardians had just arrived, to Spokane to be with his mother.

The surgery was a success, and since then, Windy and Kyle's father, Paul Manzardo, have gotten to watch their son live without the cloud of Windy's condition hanging over their lives.

"I guess now that it's over, I've kind of realized how heavy that was to just be carrying it around with you for a couple of years now," Kyle Manzardo said via MLB.com. "Just grateful it's hopefully done with now."

At the All-Star break, Manzardo was batting .215 through 87 games with seven doubles, one triple, 10 home runs and 30 RBI along with 32 runs scored for the 51-46 Cleveland Guardians, a half a game back from the Chicago White Sox in the American League Central division as of Friday.

Ryan Walker — RHP — San Francisco Giants

Joining Manzardo as one of two Cougs actively in the majors, Ryan Walker has remained a fixture of the San Francisco Giants bullpen.

However, he saw an early role as closer fade away after three saves to three blown saves. With 26 1/3 innings pitched through 27 appearances, Walker has posted a 7.52 ERA with 19 strikeouts to 15 walks.

Ian Hamilton — RHP — Atlanta Braves — Triple-A

Ian Hamilton spent the last three years in the big leagues with the New York Yankees. He signed with the Atlanta Braves' org in the offseason and, in four big-league appearances with the Braves, allowed four earned runs on six hits.

In 22 appearances for Triple-A Gwinnett, Hamilton has pitched 23 2/3 innings and is 2-0 with four saves, two holds, 30 strikeouts and a 3.04 ERA.

Brandon White — RHP — Miami Marlins — Triple-A

From Chehalis, Wash., Brandon White has been working his way up the Miami Marlins' farm system ranks since the club drafted him out of WSU in 2021.

This year with the Triple-A Jacksonville Jumbo Shrimp, White has pitched 71 2/3 innings with 16 appearances and 14 starts. He has struck out 87 batters and posted a 4.40 ERA.

Jonah Advincula — OF — Cleveland Guardians — Double-A

After starting the season injured, Jonah Advincula has joined Double-A Akron for nine games, going 9-for-28 at the plate with three doubles, three home runs, four stolen bases and 10 runs scored.

Sam Brown — INF — Washington Nationals — Double-A

Acquired by the Nationals as part of a July 30, 2025, trade with the Angels, Brown is hitting .238 with 49 hits through 56 games. He has 11 doubles, three home runs, 17 RBI and 19 runs.

Zane Mills — RHP — Chicago Cubs — Double-A

Before suffering a season-ending knee injury, Zane Mills made one appearance for Double-A Knoxville in the Chicago Cubs' organization.

Dakota Hawkins — RHP — New York Mets — High-A

Dakota Hawkins made one appearance each in the Double-A and Triple-A levels of the New York Mets' org, but has settled in with High-A Brooklyn, where through 17 appearances he is 3-0 with two holds, a 2.54 ERA and 33 strikeouts.

Will Cresswell — C — Toronto Blue Jays — Single-A

Cresswells's last 12 months have been eventful. The WSU's most-recent pro wrapped up the 2025 season in Pullman and was drafted by the Toronto Blue Jays in July 2025. He spent March representing Great Britain in the World Baseball Classic.

With the Blue Jays' Single-A affiliate, Creswell has played in 34 games and notched 23 hits, including three doubles, a home run along with 15 RBI and 11 runs.

Connor Wilford — RHP — Seattle Mariners — ACL

Connor Wilford started the year with Lexington of the Atlantic League, an independent baseball league, before the Seattle Mariners purchased his contract. Now with the Arizona Complex League Mariners, Wilford is off to a fast start, making four starts in five appearances for a 2-1 record, 2.45 ERA and 27 strikeouts through 22 innings.

While not associated with an MLB organization, left-handed pitcher A.J. Block and catcher Jacob Morrow play in independent leagues. Block hurls for a Lake County (Wis.) team, while Morrow plays for a Lincoln (Neb.) squad.

Block was with the Angels' Double-A affiliate last season and pitched 14 games for Durango in the Mexican League, recording 14 strikeouts through 8 2/3 innings.
